name: Sci-Fantasy Defence
short_description: Combine physics-based traps, weapons and magic to defend against horde of thousands enemies in this roguelike tower defense with procedurally generated levels and switching between building/combat. Do it solo in VR or with up to 4 players in one PC through local co-op.
detailed_description:
Sci-Fantasy Defence is a combination of tower defense, first/third-person shooter and slasher. Survive and protect a brain from zombies and mutants. Use advanced technologies to place traps, construct buildings and craft weapons. Buff weapons with elemental potions. Dodge enemies and attack in slow motion. 

  • There are 11 classes: stormtrooper, swordsman, telekinetic, defender, warrior, electrician, exploder, engineer, sniper, destroyer and deathbeam-man. Every class has his own play style, perks and other advantages.

  • Different types of traps, such as those that deal damage or knock enemies into the air. Buildings can be placed not only on the ground, but also on the ceiling and side foundations.

  • Different types of weapons that can be upgraded.

  • Build and upgrade turrets.

Split screen Co-op / Non-VR support

Play with friends, don't need to wait each other. There are always a free display and keyboard with mouse in PCVR, use it for the second player. Use gamedpads for extra players. Supports up to 4 desktop players. Also if you haven't got VR, then you can play it in non-VR mode (either, solo or co-op). Desktop players are full-fledged and have the same capabilities as a VR player, with the exception of some VR-exclusive features.

Multiplayer Co-op on the same computer (play online using Steam Remote Play):

  • one player in VR and the others using the monitor with keyboard/mouse and gamepads (up to 5 players)

  • [non-VR] split screen with mouse/keyboard and gamepads (up to 4 players)
